article_title: FAA Finalizes Special Conditions for ZeroAvia's 600 kW Electric Engine, Charting a Certification Path for Hydrogen-Electric Aviation

content_preview.title: FAA Finalizes Special Conditions for ZeroAvia's 600 kW Electric Engine, Charting a Certification Path for Hydrogen-Electric Aviation
content_preview.summary: The FAA has published bespoke airworthiness rules for ZeroAvia's ZA601 electric motor, marking the first time the agency has established a regulatory framework for hydrogen-electric aircraft propulsion.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Federal Aviation Administration has finalized special conditions for ZeroAvia's Model ZA601 electric engine, establishing for the first time a set of bespoke airworthiness rules tailored to hydrogen-electric aircraft propulsion. The rule, which took effect on March 18, 2026, addresses safety requirements for the 600-kilowatt electric motor at the heart of ZeroAvia's ZA600 hydrogen-electric powertrain, according to [Aviation International News](https://www.ainonline.com/aviation-...

editor_notes.source_verification: AIN Online confirms FAA 33 special conditions, ZA601 600kW, March 18 publication, certification timeline, 50% workforce cuts, delayed timeline. 5 aviation-specialist sources provide strong coverage.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims verified via AIN Online. Timeline (May 2024 application, Feb 2025 basis, Aug 2025 proposed, March 2026 final) confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced — covers both the milestone and the setbacks honestly.
